question: Explain the role of the Ultimate Heat Sink through the text and the table in Chapter 1.

nuscale_ch01_0558.title: DSRS 12.3-12.4, Rev 0: Radiation Protection Design FeaturesII.9 RG 8.15
nuscale_ch01_0558.summary: This section summarizes the NuScale Final Safety Analysis Report’s conformance with various regulatory guides related to radiation protection design features, as outlined in DSRS 12.3-12.4, Rev 0. It details whether each regulatory guide is applicable, and if so, whether NuScale’s design fully or partially conforms, with references to relevant report sections for further information. Several guides are marked as not applicable, while others are noted as partially or fully conforming.

nuscale_ch01_0557.title: DSRS 12.3-12.4, Rev 0: Radiation Protection Design FeaturesII.8 RG 8.10
nuscale_ch01_0557.summary: This section summarizes NuScale’s conformance with various regulatory guides related to radiation protection design features, as outlined in DSRS 12.3-12.4. It details whether each regulatory guide is applicable, fully conformed to, or only partially conformed to, with references to supporting sections or tables for further information. Some guides are marked as not applicable, while others are partially or fully met, demonstrating NuScale’s approach to regulatory compliance in radiation protection.

nuscale_ch01_0331.title: SRP 9.2.5, Rev 3: Ultimate Heat SinkII.4 Cooling Water System Inspection
nuscale_ch01_0331.summary: The NuScale design fully conforms to SRP 9.2.5, Rev 3, Section II.4 requirements for Cooling Water System Inspection, with no exceptions or additional comments noted. This indicates that the inspection criteria for the ultimate heat sink's cooling water system are met as specified in the regulatory guidance.

nuscale_ch01_0043.title: Ultimate Heat Sink
nuscale_ch01_0043.summary: The Ultimate Heat Sink section describes a large, stainless steel-lined, reinforced concrete pool located below plant grade, which serves as the final heat removal system for the NuScale reactor. In normal operations, heat is removed via a cooling system and external heat sink, while in accident scenarios with total AC power loss, decay heat is passively transferred to the pool, which has sufficient water inventory to ensure continued cooling through natural circulation and boiling.

nuscale_ch01_0121.title: 1.9 Conformance with Regulatory Criteria
nuscale_ch01_0121.summary: This section outlines how the NuScale Final Safety Analysis Report demonstrates compliance with applicable regulatory criteria, using a series of tables to assess conformance with various NRC regulations and guidance documents effective six months prior to the anticipated docket date. It also specifies that any applicant referencing the NuScale design must review and address regulatory conformance for site-specific and operational aspects, and details the process for evaluating and justifying any deviations from NRC Regulatory Guides.

nuscale_ch01_0122.title: 1.9.1 Conformance with Regulatory Guides
nuscale_ch01_0122.summary: Section 1.9.1 describes how NuScale evaluates and documents its compliance with NRC regulatory guides in effect six months prior to the Final Safety Analysis Report (FSAR) submission. Table 1.9-2 summarizes this conformance, including any deviations from the guidance and justifications for alternative approaches. This ensures that the design meets current regulatory expectations and transparently addresses any differences.

nuscale_ch01_0301.title: DSRS 9.1.3, Rev 0: Spent Fuel Pool Cooling and Cleanup SystemII.3 Specific Criteria to Meet GDC 5
nuscale_ch01_0301.summary: The section "DSRS 9.1.3, Rev 0: Spent Fuel Pool Cooling and Cleanup System II.3 Specific Criteria to Meet GDC 5" states that the NuScale design fully conforms to the requirements of General Design Criterion (GDC) 5, with no exceptions or additional notes. This indicates that the system meets the necessary regulatory criteria for sharing of structures, systems, and components important to safety among nuclear power units.

edges.description: Section A and Section B both list conformance with regulatory guides for radiation protection design features, and both reference Table 1.9-2 in Chapter 1 for further details, indicating that the table structurally supports the text by providing specific information about regulatory compliance relevant to the Ultimate Heat Sink.

Section A references Table 1.9-2 for details on regulatory guidance conformance, while Section B provides the specific conformance information for the Ultimate Heat Sink in that table, thus Section A structurally references Section B for supporting details.

Section B describes the design and function of the Ultimate Heat Sink, which is referenced in Section A as part of the plant's conformance with regulatory criteria for radiation protection and safety analysis.

Section A references regulatory guidance and conformance tables that include cross-references to Table 1.9-2, which also addresses the Ultimate Heat Sink discussed in Section B, thereby connecting the radiation protection design features to the cooling water system and Ultimate Heat Sink requirements through shared regulatory criteria and conformance documentation.

Section A references regulatory guidance and conformance tables that include requirements for radiation protection design features, while Section B describes the Ultimate Heat Sink's design and operation, which is a system whose safety and radiation protection functions are evaluated against the criteria and guidance cited in Section A.

Section A references the regulatory criteria and inspection requirements for the Ultimate Heat Sink, while Section B provides the specific design description of the Ultimate Heat Sink system in the NuScale plant, thus Section B specifies the technical implementation that Section A evaluates for regulatory conformance.

Section A introduces the conformance tables and legends, while Section B provides specific entries from those tables showing how the design conforms to regulatory guides, thus Section B is a detailed instance of the framework described in Section A.

Section A introduces the conformance tables and their legends, while Section B provides specific entries from those tables showing how the design conforms (or partially conforms) to regulatory guides, thus Section B provides detailed evidence for the framework described in Section A.

Section A introduces the conformance tables and legends that are used to assess compliance with regulatory criteria, while Section B provides the specific conformance assessment for the Ultimate Heat Sink as referenced in those tables.

Section A introduces and explains the use of conformance tables and legends, which are then applied in Section B to indicate the NuScale design's conformance status with specific regulatory guides, thus Section A provides the structural framework for interpreting the data presented in Section B.

Section A introduces the tables (including Table 1.9-2) that summarize conformance with regulatory guides, while Section B provides specific entries from those tables showing the conformance status for various radiation protection design features, thus Section B provides detailed data that Section A structurally organizes and references.

Section A introduces the tables that assess conformance with regulatory criteria, while Section B provides the specific conformance status for the Ultimate Heat Sink as evaluated in those tables, directly connecting the general process to a concrete example.

Section A describes the Ultimate Heat Sink (UHS) as a seismic Category I system essential for spent fuel cooling and shielding during accident conditions, while Section B references regulatory guidance and conformance related to radiation protection design features, which are supported by the UHS's role in accident mitigation as described in Section A.

Section A describes the Ultimate Heat Sink's (UHS) role as a seismic Category I supply system for spent fuel cooling and shielding during accident conditions, while Section B references regulatory guidance and conformance related to radiation protection design features, which are supported by the UHS's function in mitigating radiological risks during such events.

Section A describes how the Ultimate Heat Sink (UHS) serves as a seismic Category I supply system for spent fuel cooling during accident conditions, while Section B outlines the regulatory criteria and inspection requirements for the UHS, thus Section A's design details are intended to satisfy the regulatory requirements specified in Section B.

Section A provides an overview of conformance with regulatory criteria and references the tables, while Section B specifically addresses conformance with regulatory guides using those same tables, connecting them through the use of Table 1.9-1 and Table 1.9-2.

Section A provides a summary table indicating the conformance status of the Ultimate Heat Sink (UHS) system with regulatory criteria, while Section B details the specific regulatory requirements and the extent to which the UHS system meets those requirements, particularly regarding seismic and quality group classifications.

Section A provides the framework and tables for assessing conformance with regulatory criteria, while Section B presents the specific conformance status and details regarding the Ultimate Heat Sink's role in meeting those criteria, directly utilizing the structure and codes defined in Section A.
